notes:
Opening opportunity work notes. Record inspected with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence. Visible record contains only ev_e61df84283cc_market-page, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17, size 4971, system case_packet. Read exact contents via read_evidence_range. Packet proves the market rule and deadline: Iran initiated; major closure; not solely weather; by May 24, 2026 11:59 PM ET; major closure includes broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting/arriving/departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region; complete non-weather suspension of commercial arrivals/departures affecting at least two of IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN qualifies; primary resolution sources official Iranian aviation authorities and consensus credible reporting. Packet does not prove occurrence/scope/reason of any May 2026 closure.

Source map before opening: decisive elements are Iranian initiation, timing before deadline, non-weather/security cause, major scope, and source quality. Strongest expected proof: official NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 and related Iranian CAA/aviation authority material; confirming sources from credible reporting and aviation tracking/risk databases; adverse checks on whether the measure was merely partial because some airports continued limited daylight operations and whether listed market airports were actually affected.

Native investigation performed for theory/source targeting only, because opening does not allow evidence admission. Searches: Iran closes airspace May 24 2026 commercial flights; site:reuters.com Iran airspace May 24 2026 closure flights; Iran Civil Aviation Organization airspace closed May 24 2026 flights; OIIX A1010/26 Iran NOTAM May 22 2026 western Tehran FIR airports; May 22 May 25 2026 Iran Civil Aviation Authority NOTAM western airspace. Leads found: AbdiMedia article at https://abdimedia.net/en/economic/notam; Arab Times at https://www.arabtimesonline.com/news/iran-suspends-flight-permissions-restricts-western-airspace-access/; SafeAirspace Iran page https://safeairspace.net/iran/; search-result lead to Indian Express quiz text. These leads generally describe NOTAM A1010/26 closing all airports in western Tehran FIR except limited listed airports, valid May 22-May 25, with prior permissions canceled and fresh CAA approval required. They are not yet admitted record evidence and should not be cited as support until submitted in a later evidence phase.

Unresolved gaps for next phase: capture official NOTAM/source if accessible; submit source material before relying on it; map airport codes and listed market airports; verify exact effective times against May 24 11:59 PM ET; determine whether at least two listed airports had complete suspension or whether the broader major-region theory is stronger; look for credible adverse/corrective reporting and official later reopening statements.

Defendant opening work notes: Inspected current record with get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Only visible evidence is case-packet ev_e61df84283cc_market-page, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17, 4971 bytes. It establishes market rule/deadline/source preference/examples, but not any May 22-25 closure event. Source map: decisive primary source likely operative Iranian NOTAM or CAA/Iran Airports material, reported as OIIX A1010/26, May 22-May 25. Web search leads (not record evidence) found Indian Express/Gulf News/AbdiMedia/social snippets describing western Tehran FIR airports closed with exceptions/daylight operations, previous permissions revoked/new approvals required. Adverse checks: regional western FIR only; exceptions reportedly include several airports; daylight operations may continue; exact transit/arrivals/departures language and listed airport mapping unresolved; source snippets are not evidence. Opening theory: concede rule, dispute proof and scope. Plaintiff must produce official source chain and prove Iran-initiated, non-weather, timely, major/general commercial closure or complete suspension at two listed airports. Current record does not do so. Durable notes written to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Merits argument work notes. Re-inspected record at start of opportunity with get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Before plaintiff submissions, record contained market page ev_e61df84283cc_market-page and both openings only. Market page proves rule and deadline but not event occurrence.

Source map: decisive elements are Iranian initiation, timing before May 24 23:59 ET, non-weather cause, and major-region/broad closure scope. Strongest source target found: NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 and superseding A1015/26. Official CAA site/source not directly captured; Notamify preserves raw NOTAM text. Credible reporting used to corroborate CAA attribution and non-weather security context.

Evidence submitted and accepted: ev_a95b8d7e6e97_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-a95b8d7e6e97 (Notamify A1010/26 raw NOTAM); ev_ecbf01d69176_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-ecbf01d69176 (Notamify A1015/26 raw NOTAM replacing A1010/26); ev_a6be31686c80_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-a6be31686c80 (Indian Express report); ev_098dc1dc0d2d_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-098dc1dc0d2d (Arab Times report).

Analysis: A1010/26: A)OIIX B)2605222046 C)2605250830EST, all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except named airports operating sunrise-sunset; all previous permissions suspended; new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights. A1015/26 replaced A1010/26 from 2605231821 through same C time, expanding exceptions but retaining closure/permission regime. NOTAM C field EST treated as estimated, not Eastern Standard Time. May 24 23:59 ET equals May 25 03:59 UTC, so the NOTAM series remained in force at the deadline.

Argument choice: rely on the market rule's major Iranian airspace region path, not primarily the two-listed-airport complete-suspension path. Raw NOTAM supports closure of all airports in the west part of Tehran FIR subject to limited exceptions and approval conditions; rule says limited exceptions to a broad closure do not disqualify it. Secondary reporting supports CAA attribution, western-half/region framing, and non-weather security context. Caveats: Notamify is not direct official CAA custody; Indian Express airport list differs from raw NOTAM, so use it for corroboration/context, not exact raw airport list. Defendant likely partial-closure argument answered by broad regional closure plus suspended prior permissions/fresh CAA approval requirement.

Defendant merits work notes: Re-inspected current record and new plaintiff evidence. Used list/stat/read on ev_a95b8d7e6e97 (A1010/26), ev_ecbf01d69176 (A1015/26), ev_a6be31686c80 (Indian Express), ev_098dc1dc0d2d (Arab Times), plus case packet. Key point: A1010/A1015 are categorized AD Airport Operations and text repeatedly says all airports in west part of Tehran FIR are closed except named airports operational sunrise to sunset; previous permissions suspended and new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights. A1015 expands exceptions to 12 airports. Indian Express says operational airports included multiple listed-market airports (Mehrabad, Imam Khomeini, Isfahan, Shiraz, Mashhad) subject to daylight and fresh approval. Defense argument: do not contest timing/Iran/non-weather as main issues; attack market-defined major closure. Plaintiff relies on major-region path but raw NOTAM does not establish a general commercial-transit airspace closure and contains substantial operational exceptions. Listed-airport complete suspension path is undermined by continued limited operations at listed airports. Custody weakness remains: no official Iranian CAA source in record; Notamify is third-party/inactive page, secondary reports corroborate but do not replace official source. No new defendant evidence submitted because plaintiff exhibits contain the strongest defense admissions. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff rebuttal notes. Re-inspected record with get_case/list_evidence/stat for A1010/A1015. No new defense evidence was submitted; defendant relied on plaintiff exhibits and a defense technical report. Strongest defense points: A1010/A1015 are AD Airport Operations rather than explicit FIR-wide overflight prohibitions; exceptions/daylight operations and fresh approval make the measure partial; plaintiff is not proving the two-listed-airport complete-suspension path; Notamify is third-party custody.

No new evidence submitted for rebuttal because the answer is in existing admitted rule text and NOTAM wording. Rebuttal strategy: emphasize plaintiff does not rely on two-listed-airport complete-suspension path. The major-region clause allows broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights in a major Iranian airspace region and expressly says limited exceptions, including CAA pre-approved flights, do not disqualify. Raw A1010/A1015 say ALL AIRPORTS in west part of Tehran FIR are closed except enumerated daylight operations; all previous permissions suspended for all operators; new CAA/Iran CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights. The approval mechanism is the exception structure, not proof there was no closure. Defendant conceded serious Iranian non-weather restriction through deadline.

Caution: avoid overclaiming complete overflight ban. Use Arab Times/Indian Express only as corroboration for western airspace/sector and CAA attribution. Main argument: market text includes arriving/departing as well as transiting, and all airports in a major region closed with limited exceptions is enough under preponderance.

Defendant surrebuttal notes: Re-scanned record with get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff rebuttal submitted no new evidence; it relies on existing exhibits. Rebuttal points: major-region path only; limited exceptions/preapproval contemplated by market rule; AD Airport Operations label not dispositive; April 2024 western-airspace example supports regional closures; provenance weakness goes only to weight. Response plan: accept that limited exceptions can coexist with a broad closure, but broad/general commercial-flight closure must first be proven. The admitted raw NOTAMs still say all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except named airports operational sunrise-to-sunset; they do not expressly show commercial transit/overflight suspension or complete arrivals/departures suspension at listed airports. A1015 expanded exceptions and reporting of multiple market-listed airports operational under daylight approvals undercut complete-suspension path and make major-region path dependent on unproven scope. April 2024 example does not transform a western airport-permission regime into a qualifying airspace closure. No new evidence submitted; rely on existing DX/PX evidence.

Closing notes. Final record scan completed with get_case/list_evidence/stat. No new evidence after plaintiff rebuttal; defendant surrebuttal added argument only. Closing cannot submit evidence or reports.

Decisive record: PX-1 market rule; PX-2 A1010/26 raw NOTAM capture; PX-3 A1015/26 superseding raw NOTAM capture; PX-4 Indian Express report; PX-5 Arab Times report. Defendant concedes, or effectively does not contest, Iranian initiation, non-weather/security context, and timing through the deadline; dispute is scope and provenance.

Closing theory: preponderance does not require perfect official CAA custody. The rule separately allows a broad closure/cancellation/suspension in a major Iranian airspace region and says limited exceptions, including CAA pre-approval, do not disqualify. A1010/A1015 say all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except enumerated sunrise-to-sunset operations, all prior permissions suspended, and new CAA permission required. The measure remained effective past May 24 23:59 ET. Secondary reports corroborate CAA attribution and non-weather/security context. Weakness: no direct official CAA database and no explicit overflight-ban language; answer is that market text includes arriving/departing as well as transiting and treats western-regional closures as potentially qualifying.

Defendant closing notes: Closing opportunity scanned with get_case/list_evidence. No new evidence since argument phase; plaintiff closing adds no evidence. Plaintiff's final theory: timing/Iran/non-weather are strong; A1010/A1015 ran through deadline; major-region path only; all airports in west Tehran FIR closed with controlled exceptions; AD/overflight point not dispositive because arrivals/departures enough; April 2024 example; provenance sufficient. Defense closing plan: concede serious Iranian non-weather restriction and timing. Burden remains on plaintiff to prove market-defined major closure, not merely serious airport restrictions. Decisive record facts: no official CAA source; Notamify third-party/inactive; NOTAMs categorized AD Airport Operations; operative language repeatedly says airports; many listed airports operational sunrise-to-sunset with fresh permissions; Indian Express says multiple listed-market airports operational; no official map/traffic evidence proving western Tehran FIR as major region or general commercial transit suspension. Answer strongest contrary point: limited exceptions clause only applies after broad closure is proven; April 2024 example shows some western closures can qualify, not that this one does. Ask council to find proposition not proven.
